message:
Add unit tests for doctor diagnostics (git_safety_guard-1gt.7.1)

Added 21 unit tests covering:
- HookDiagnostics struct validation (healthy/unhealthy states)
- ConfigDiagnostics struct validation (errors/warnings)
- is_valid_pack_id() pack ID validation (core, categories, invalid)
- JSON parsing for settings.json hook detection
- is_dcg_command() recognition of various command forms
- run_smoke_test() verifying evaluator functionality

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -27,7 +27,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.6","title":"Implement dcg explain CLI subcommand","description":"## Objective

Implement the `dcg explain <command>` CLI subcommand that provides a full decision trace for any command.

## Background

When DCG blocks (or allows) a command, users and developers need to understand WHY:
- Which patterns matched?
- Which packs were evaluated?
- Was it blocked by a destructive pattern or allowed by a safe pattern?
- What context classification was applied?

The explain mode provides complete transparency into DCG's decision-making process.

## CLI Interface

```bash
# Basic usage
dcg explain "git reset --hard HEAD~5"

# Output format options
dcg explain "command" --format pretty   # Human-readable (default)
dcg explain "command" --format json     # Machine-readable
dcg explain "command" --format compact  # Single-line summary

# Verbose mode (include all evaluated packs, not just matching)
dcg explain "command" --verbose
```

## Decision Trace Structure

The explain output should show the complete decision pipeline:

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  DCG Decision Trace                                              ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Input: git reset --hard HEAD~5                                  ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  1. Parse                                                        ║
║     └─ Tool: Bash                                                ║
║     └─ Command: git reset --hard HEAD~5                          ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  2. Quick Reject                                                 ║
║     └─ Keywords found: git                                       ║
║     └─ Result: CONTINUE (keywords present)                       ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  3. Normalization                                                ║
║     └─ Original: git reset --hard HEAD~5                         ║
║     └─ Normalized: git reset --hard HEAD~5                       ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  4. Context Classification                                       ║
║     └─ Span[0..25]: Executed                                     ║
║     └─ No safe-string-arg context detected                       ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  5. Heredoc Detection                                            ║
║     └─ Tier 1 trigger: NO                                        ║
║     └─ Result: SKIP heredoc analysis                             ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  6. Pack Evaluation                                              ║
║     └─ Enabled packs: core.git, core.filesystem                  ║
║     └─ Pack: core.git                                            ║
║        ├─ Safe pattern check: NO MATCH                           ║
║        └─ Destructive pattern check:                             ║
║           └─ MATCH: reset-hard                                   ║
║              Pattern: git reset --hard                           ║
║              Reason: Hard reset can permanently lose commits     ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  7. Decision                                                     ║
║     └─ Result: DENY                                              ║
║     └─ Rule ID: core.git:reset-hard                              ║
║     └─ Reason: Hard reset can permanently lose commits           ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  8. Suggestions                                                  ║
║     └─ Run `git diff` first to see what would be lost            ║
║     └─ Use `git reset --soft` to preserve changes                ║
║     └─ To allow: dcg allow core.git:reset-hard --reason "..."    ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## JSON Output Format

```json
{
  "input": {
    "raw": "git reset --hard HEAD~5",
    "tool": "Bash"
  },
  "trace": {
    "quick_reject": {
      "keywords_found": ["git"],
      "result": "continue"
    },
    "normalization": {
      "original": "git reset --hard HEAD~5",
      "normalized": "git reset --hard HEAD~5"
    },
    "context_classification": {
      "spans": [
        {"range": [0, 25], "kind": "executed"}
      ]
    },
    "heredoc_detection": {
      "tier1_triggered": false
    },
    "pack_evaluation": {
      "enabled_packs": ["core.git", "core.filesystem"],
      "matches": [
        {
          "pack_id": "core.git",
          "pattern_name": "reset-hard",
          "pattern": "git reset --hard",
          "reason": "Hard reset can permanently lose commits"
        }
      ]
    }
  },
  "decision": "deny",
  "rule_id": "core.git:reset-hard",
  "reason": "Hard reset can permanently lose commits",
  "suggestions": [
    {
      "kind": "preview_first",
      "text": "Run `git diff` first to see what would be lost"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Compact Output Format

```
DENY core.git:reset-hard "git reset --hard HEAD~5" -> Hard reset can permanently lose commits
```

## Implementation Notes

- Reuse the shared evaluator from 99e.3.1
- Add tracing/instrumentation hooks to capture each step
- Store trace in a `DecisionTrace` struct as evaluation proceeds
- Format the trace based on `--format` flag

## Performance

Explain mode is NOT on the hot path (user explicitly requests it), so:
- Can be slower than normal evaluation
- Can allocate for trace storage
- Should still complete in <100ms for typical commands

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg explain` works for all command types (simple, heredoc, pipes)
- Pretty format is human-readable and helps users understand decisions
- JSON format is valid and parseable by external tools
- Compact format is suitable for logging/scripting
- Suggestions are included in the output","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:51.003342694-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T21:55:30.329440887-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-07T21:55:30.329440887-05:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of git_safety_guard-1gt.1 + subtasks; superseded and split for better dependency tracking.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:51.004688188-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7","title":"Improve dcg doctor (installation + config + hook health)","description":"## Objective

Make `dcg doctor` a *high-trust* diagnostic that rapidly answers:

- "Is dcg installed correctly and on PATH?"
- "Is the Claude Code hook registered correctly (exact matcher/command path)?"
- "Is configuration loading what I think it is (and is it valid)?"
- "If something is wrong, what exact command fixes it?"

This is an adoption lever: a guard that is hard to diagnose gets disabled.

## Current state (baseline)

`dcg doctor` exists (see `src/cli.rs`) and already performs basic checks, but it does not yet validate many real-world failure modes:

- wrong hook wiring (wrong tool matcher, wrong command, multiple hooks)
- config parse errors / unknown pack IDs
- cross-platform settings path nuances
- "it works in CLI but not in hook" mismatch scenarios

## Proposed doctor contract

### Output

- Human-readable by default.
- Optional machine-readable mode (JSON) for CI/automation/support.

### Checks (v1)

1) **Binary presence + version**
- Verify `dcg` resolves via PATH.
- Print version + build metadata.

2) **Claude Code settings discovery**
- Locate settings file reliably across platforms.
- If missing: warn-only (Claude Code may not be configured yet).

3) **Hook registration validation**
- Verify `hooks.PreToolUse` contains a `matcher: Bash` entry with a dcg hook.
- Detect duplicates and ambiguous entries.
- Validate that the configured hook command points to an executable (especially if absolute).

4) **Config discovery + parse + validation**
- Locate `~/.config/dcg/config.toml` (and any future supported locations).
- If present, parse and validate:
  - unknown pack IDs
  - conflicting enable/disable
  - overrides with invalid regex (once overrides are precompiled)

5) **Self-check smoke test (optional)**
- Prove the evaluator works end-to-end:
  - allow case (e.g., `git status`)
  - deny case (e.g., `git reset --hard`)

### Fix mode (`--fix`)

- Safe, explicit, non-destructive fixes only:
  - install hook if missing
  - create a sample config if missing (refuse to overwrite unless explicitly forced)
- Never delete or overwrite user files.

### Post-allowlist extension

Once allowlists ship, doctor should additionally validate allowlist presence/validity. This is tracked separately in `git_safety_guard-1gt.7.4` so the rest of doctor can ship earlier.

## Acceptance criteria

- Doctor catches and explains the top 5 real install/config/hook wiring failure modes.
- `dcg doctor --fix` is idempotent and performs only safe changes.
- Output is actionable (copy/paste next step).
- Tests cover settings parsing + hook detection on representative fixtures.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:00:02.674578124-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:14:06.18244138-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:00:11.307939914-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.1","title":"Doctor: expand checks (settings, hook wiring, config validation)","description":"## Objective

Implement the concrete `dcg doctor` checks described in `git_safety_guard-1gt.7`.

## Detailed requirements

### Hook wiring validation

- Parse `~/.claude/settings.json` (or platform-specific equivalent) and verify:
  - `hooks.PreToolUse` exists
  - at least one entry with `matcher: "Bash"`
  - within that entry, at least one hook with `type: "command"` and `command: "dcg"` (or an absolute path to dcg)
- Detect and warn on:
  - multiple dcg hooks (duplicate execution)
  - hooks registered for the wrong matcher/tool
  - hooks pointing to a missing executable

### Config validation

- Confirm which config file is in use.
- If config exists, validate:
  - TOML parses cleanly
  - enabled/disabled pack sets are coherent
  - pack IDs exist in the registry (unknown pack → warning/error)
  - override regex compilation failures are surfaced clearly

### Output

- Each check prints a concise status line and, on failure, a short remediation.

## Acceptance criteria

- Doctor distinguishes between "not installed" vs "installed but not hooked" vs "hooked but misconfigured".
- Error messages include exact next step commands.

## Tests

- Add fixture settings.json files that cover:
  - missing hooks
  - wrong matcher
  - duplicate hooks
  - absolute path hook
- Unit tests validate detection logic against fixtures.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:00:29.816030738-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T23:15:45.997659396-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:00:39.518546237-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.2","title":"Doctor: safe fix mode (install hook + init config without overwrite)","description":"## Objective

Make `dcg doctor --fix` actually useful while staying **non-destructive**.

## Requirements

### Safe fixes allowed

- If hook is missing: run the same logic as `dcg install`.
- If config is missing: create a sample config *only if the path does not exist*.

### Explicitly forbidden

- Overwriting or deleting any existing user file.
- Making irreversible changes without explicit user confirmation.

### UX

- When `--fix` is supplied, doctor prints what it is about to do, then proceeds.
- If a fix cannot be applied safely (e.g., file exists), print a clear instruction.

## Acceptance criteria

- Running `dcg doctor --fix` on a fresh machine registers the hook and creates config.
- Running it on a configured machine makes no changes.
- The command is idempotent.

## Tests

- Integration tests in a temp HOME directory:
  - missing settings/config → fix creates them
  - existing settings/config → fix does not overwrite

- Running it on a configured machine makes no changes.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.3","title":"Doctor: machine-readable output (--format json)","description":"## Objective

Add an optional JSON output mode for `dcg doctor` so automation and support workflows can consume diagnostics.

## Requirements

- CLI: `dcg doctor --format json` (or `--json`).
- JSON includes:
  - `schema_version`
  - list of checks `{ id, status, message, remediation? }`
  - detected paths (config path, settings path) with redaction as needed
- Exit codes:
  - 0: all good
  - 1: issues found
  - 2: fatal error (e.g., cannot read settings due to permissions)

## Acceptance criteria

- JSON schema is stable and snapshot-tested.
- Human-readable output remains unchanged by default.

## Notes

This is P3 because it is not required for core adoption, but it reduces friction for org-scale rollout.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Determinism
`dcg doctor --format json` must be deterministic so it can be used in scripts/CI:

- stable key and list ordering
- stable version/config paths
- stable problem identifiers","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:01:14.265225432-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:29:25.066636446-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:01:23.311324539-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.4","title":"Doctor: allowlist discovery + validation checks","description":"## Objective

Extend `dcg doctor` to validate allowlist installation and health once allowlists exist.

This is intentionally split out so the rest of `dcg doctor` can ship earlier.

## Requirements

- Detect user allowlist (`~/.config/dcg/allowlist.toml`) and project allowlist (`.dcg/allowlist.toml`).
- For each allowlist found:
  - validate schema version
  - validate required fields (`rule`, `reason`, etc.)
  - detect expired entries and surface as warnings
  - detect unsafe wildcard entries and surface as warnings/errors per allowlist safety rules

## Output UX

- In human-readable mode, print a clear status line per layer:
  - `User allowlist: OK` / `MISSING` / `INVALID` + 1-line remediation
  - `Project allowlist: OK` / ...

## Acceptance criteria

- Doctor reports allowlist problems without crashing.
- Doctor guides users to:
  - `dcg allowlist validate --project|--user`
  - `dcg allowlist add ...`

## Tests

- Fixture allowlist files for:
  - valid entry
  - invalid schema
  - expired entry
  - unsafe wildcard missing `risk_ack`
- Unit tests assert doctor classification matches fixtures.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.2` (allowlists) must exist first.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:13:25.667664777-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:13:25.667664777-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:13:35.204041564-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:13:40.356612088-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.5","title":"Doctor: detect observe-mode expiry and recommend tightening","description":"## Objective

Prevent a common rollout failure mode: users enable warn-first observe mode and forget to tighten.

`dcg doctor` should detect when observe mode is enabled (and/or expired) and provide clear guidance.

## Requirements

- If `policy.default_mode` is warn/log-only globally, doctor prints:
  - current mode
  - whether an `observe_until` is set
  - whether it is expired

- If expired:
  - doctor warns prominently
  - prints a suggested config change to tighten (or remove observe)

## Acceptance criteria

- Doctor output makes it obvious when the guard is not enforcing.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.3.3` (observe mode config)
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:18:00.275844431-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:18:00.275844431-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:18:10.822182359-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:18:15.86285868-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -59,7 +59,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-99e.1.1","title":"Implement pack-aware global quick reject","description":"## Objective

Fix the hook pipeline so that enabled non-core packs are evaluated when their keywords appear in the command.

## Background

The current hook pipeline returns early when the command contains neither `git` nor `rm`, which prevents packs like Docker/Kubernetes/DB from ever running.

See parent bug for reproduction.

## Root Cause Locations

- `src/main.rs:587`: Early return condition only checks `git`/`rm`
- `src/packs/mod.rs:383`: `global_quick_reject` only checks for substrings `git` and `rm`

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Simple Keyword Union (MVP)

1. Load config (already done in hook mode) and compute `enabled_pack_ids()`.
2. Expand enabled categories to concrete pack IDs (use `PackRegistry::expand_enabled`).
3. Build a **keyword union** from all enabled packs' `Pack.keywords`.
4. Replace the current early-return condition with:
   - "return early only if no enabled pack keywords exist in the command"

### Suggested Algorithm

```rust
/// Returns true if the command can be safely skipped (no relevant keywords)
fn pack_aware_quick_reject(cmd: &str, enabled_keywords: &[&str]) -> bool {
    // Fast path: use memchr::memmem for each keyword
    for kw in enabled_keywords {
        if memchr::memmem::find(cmd.as_bytes(), kw.as_bytes()).is_some() {
            return false;  // Keyword found, must evaluate packs
        }
    }
    true  // No keywords found, safe to skip
}
```

### Phase 2: Aho-Corasick Optimization (if needed)

If the keyword union grows large (>20 keywords), replace with Aho-Corasick automaton:

```rust
use aho_corasick::AhoCorasick;

static KEYWORD_AUTOMATON: LazyLock<AhoCorasick> = LazyLock::new(|| {
    let keywords = collect_all_enabled_keywords();
    AhoCorasick::new(&keywords).expect("valid keywords")
});

fn pack_aware_quick_reject(cmd: &str) -> bool {
    !KEYWORD_AUTOMATON.is_match(cmd)
}
```

## Performance Budget

- **Target:** <10μs for quick reject on typical commands
- **Panic threshold:** >100μs (indicates regression)
- **Zero allocations** on the hot path after initialization

## Case Sensitivity

- CLI tool names (git, docker, kubectl): case-sensitive (Linux convention)
- SQL keywords (DROP, DELETE): may need both cases - defer to pack keyword definitions

## Considerations

- Keep this check separate from the legacy `quick_reject` so it's not accidentally constrained to `git`/`rm` again.
- Build the keyword union once per invocation, ideally without heap churn.
- Consider word-boundary matching in Phase 3 (see 99e.8) to avoid false triggers on substrings like "digit" containing "git".

## Acceptance Criteria

- `docker system prune` reaches `REGISTRY.check_command` when docker pack is enabled.
- `kubectl delete namespace foo` reaches `REGISTRY.check_command` when kubectl pack is enabled.
- For a command like `ls -la`, the hook returns early (no expensive regex) and remains sub-millisecond.
- Benchmark shows <10μs for quick reject path.","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Tests
- Implement/verify regression coverage in `git_safety_guard-99e.1.2`:
  - hook JSON path denies `docker system prune` when `containers.docker` enabled
  - hook JSON path denies a kubectl destructive example when `kubernetes.kubectl` enabled
  - parity with `dcg test` for the same commands
- Add a perf benchmark/regression in `git_safety_guard-99e.7` that exercises the pack-aware keyword gate on:
  - a typical allow command (no keywords)
  - a command containing an enabled-pack keyword","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:58:22.543207508-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:47:26.020247486-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-07T23:47:26.020247486-05:00","close_reason":"Implementation verified complete. pack_aware_quick_reject is working in hook mode for all enabled packs (docker/kubectl/postgresql/redis/terraform). Added 17 regression tests to e2e_test.sh covering non-core pack reachability. All 137 e2e tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.1.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:58:22.54465821-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-99e.1.2","title":"Add regression tests: hook reaches non-core packs","description":"## Objective

Add tests that would fail under the current bug (non-core packs unreachable) and pass once fixed.

## Why tests are critical

This bug is easy to reintroduce during future performance work (e.g., adding new global quick rejects). We need a permanent regression test suite that exercises:

- Hook-mode pipeline (stdin JSON) end-to-end
- Non-core pack enablement
- CLI vs hook consistency

## Test plan

### 1) Hook-mode integration test

- Build a JSON input for `tool_name=Bash` and command `docker system prune`.
- Ensure docker pack is enabled via config for the test (env var override or temp config file).
- Execute the actual binary in test harness (or call the hook pipeline function directly if exposed).
- Assert:
  - stdout contains JSON
  - `permissionDecision` is `deny`
  - pack attribution includes `containers.docker` (once deterministic metadata is implemented)

### 2) CLI `dcg test` parity test

- Run `dcg test "docker system prune" --with-packs containers.docker`.
- Assert it reports BLOCKED.
- (Optionally) ensure hook mode reports the same.

### 3) Negative control

- With docker pack enabled, `docker ps` should ALLOW.

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests fail on current main branch (before bugfix) and pass after.
- Tests run fast and do not require network.
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:58:43.071778915-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:48:07.318723517-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-07T23:48:07.318723517-05:00","close_reason":"Added 10 unit tests for pack_aware_quick_reject, pack registry, and full pipeline integration. Verified E2E tests (137 total) all pass including non-core pack tests for docker, kubectl, postgresql, redis, terraform.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.1.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:58:43.073195963-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-99e.10","title":"Hardening: global input size limits + fail-open policy (DoS resistance)","description":"## Objective

Ensure `dcg` never hangs or spikes unpredictably on pathological inputs by enforcing conservative global limits.

This is about protecting the *hook hot path* against:

- extremely large hook JSON payloads
- extremely large command strings (including multiline heredocs)
- degenerate cases that cause excessive allocation or slow matching

## Proposed limits (v1)

- Max hook input bytes (stdin): e.g., 256 KiB (configurable)
- Max command bytes after extraction: e.g., 64 KiB (configurable)
- Max heredoc bytes extracted for Tier 2/3 analysis: e.g., 32 KiB (configurable)
- Max findings / max matches per command: cap to avoid runaway reporting

## Policy (critical)

- On limit exceeded: **fail-open** (ALLOW) in hook mode, but emit structured log (if enabled) so we can tune.
- In `dcg test` / `dcg explain` / `dcg scan`, surface as an explicit "skipped due to limits" diagnostic.

## Acceptance criteria

- No panics on oversized input.
- Latency remains bounded even for worst-case long strings.
- Tests cover:
  - oversized stdin JSON
  - oversized command string
  - oversized heredoc body

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-99e.3.1` (shared evaluator) so limits apply uniformly across hook/CLI/scan.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-99e.11","title":"Hardening: regex safety audit (prefer linear-time regex where possible)","description":"## Objective

Reduce the risk of catastrophic regex backtracking and keep performance predictable by preferring a linear-time regex engine when we don't need backtracking features.

## Background

`fancy-regex` supports lookaround but is a backtracking engine. Even well-intentioned patterns can become slow on adversarial inputs.

This matters because `dcg` runs on every Bash command.

## Strategy

1) **Classify patterns**
- Identify which patterns truly require `fancy-regex` features (lookahead/lookbehind).
- For patterns that do not: migrate to the `regex` crate.

2) **Engine split**
- Provide a small internal abstraction so packs can use:
  - `regex::Regex` for linear-time patterns
  - `fancy_regex::Regex` only when needed

3) **Guardrails**
- Add micro-benchmarks for representative worst-case inputs.
- Add a regression test that runs the full evaluator against a corpus of long strings and asserts bounded runtime (coarse, not flaky).

## Acceptance criteria

- Majority of patterns use linear-time regex (where feasible).
- No functional regressions in safe/destructive matching.
- Benchmarks show no latency spikes on long inputs.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-99e.7` (core perf benchmarks) or `git_safety_guard-7tg.3` (budget enforcement)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-99e.12","title":"Normalization: strip common wrappers (sudo/env/command/\git) safely","description":"## Objective

Improve correctness and reduce bypasses/false negatives by normalizing common *wrapper prefixes* so destructive rules match consistently.

This is a direct follow-up to the migration toward a shared evaluator: normalization should be defined **once** and used by hook, test, explain, and scan.

## Motivation / examples

These should behave the same as the unwrapped command:

- `sudo git reset --hard` → should match `git reset --hard`
- `env GIT_DIR=.git git reset --hard` → should match
- `\git reset --hard` (bash alias bypass) → should match

Also important: some wrapper-like commands are *not* wrappers in certain forms:

- `command -v git` is not executing git; it is querying PATH.
- `env` without a command is just printing env.

## Design constraints

- Be conservative: only strip wrappers when syntax makes it unambiguous.
- Never change meaning of non-wrapper commands.
- Preserve the original command for explain output; also keep a normalized form.

## Proposed wrapper set (v1)

### 1) `sudo`

- Strip leading `sudo` and its options:
  - `-E`, `-H`, `-n`, `-k`, `-K`, `-S`, `-b`
  - `-u <user>`, `-g <group>`, `-h <host>`, `-p <prompt>`
  - stop parsing options at `--`

### 2) `env`

- Strip leading `env` and its option/assignment prefix:
  - options like `-i`, `-u <name>`, `--ignore-environment`
  - leading `NAME=VALUE` assignments
- Stop once we reach a non-assignment token (the real command).

### 3) Leading backslash escapes

- If the *first command token* starts with `\` (e.g., `\git`, `\rm`), strip the leading backslash.

### 4) `command` (careful)

- Only treat `command` as a wrapper when it is used to execute a command, not query:
  - `command -- git reset --hard` (wrapper)
  - `command -p git ...` (wrapper)
  - but NOT: `command -v git` / `command -V git`

## Acceptance criteria

- Wrapper-stripped commands produce identical decisions to the unwrapped form.
- Explain mode shows both raw and normalized command.
- No new false positives for `command -v`, `env` without command, etc.

## Tests

- Unit tests for each wrapper form, including edge cases and negative cases.
- E2E additions in the existing script suite (or integration tests) proving parity:
  - allow cases remain allowed
  - deny cases remain denied

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-99e.3.1` (shared evaluator) so normalization is centralized.
- `git_safety_guard-99e.3` (unify hook pipeline) to remove legacy drift.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-99e.13","title":"Config discovery optimizations (explicit env var, faster repo root detection)","description":"## Objective

Reduce overhead and confusion in config loading/discovery by making configuration paths explicit and fast.

This is primarily about *ergonomics* and *predictability*:

- Users should always be able to answer: "Which config file is dcg using?"
- Deep working directories should not incur slow upward traversal.

## Requirements (v1)

### Explicit config path override

- Support an env var override, e.g. `DCG_CONFIG=/path/to/config.toml`.
- If set:
  - use it
  - validate existence and parse errors clearly
  - surface in `dcg config` and `dcg doctor`

### Fast repo-root detection (for repo-scoped features)

Some features need repo root (safe cleanup pack, project allowlists, scan defaults).

- Implement a bounded upward search for `.git`:
  - stop at filesystem root
  - cap maximum parent traversals (e.g., 50)
  - avoid `canonicalize()` on every hop unless needed

### Determinism

- When multiple config sources exist, precedence order is explicit and documented.

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg doctor` reports the exact config path in use.
- No observable latency regression on common commands in deep trees.

## Tests

- Unit tests for config precedence:
  - env var override beats default
  - missing override path yields clear error (hook fail-open; CLI fail)
- Unit tests for repo-root detection:
  - finds `.git` correctly
  - respects traversal cap

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.7` doctor improvements (to surface which config is in use).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-99e.14","title":"Hardening: evaluation time budget + fail-open on overrun (hook safety)","description":"## Objective

Guarantee that the hook path stays responsive by enforcing a conservative evaluation time budget and **fail-open** behavior when budgets are exceeded.

## Background

`dcg` is invoked for every Bash command. A rare 200ms spike is worse than many tools' average runtime.

We cannot preempt every operation (e.g., a single regex call), but we *can*:

- design algorithms to avoid known worst-cases (see regex audit)
- cap expensive tiers (heredoc extraction/AST parsing)
- enforce stage-level deadlines and bail out early when possible

## Proposed design (v1)

- Introduce a `Deadline` helper (wraps `Instant` + max duration).
- The shared evaluator receives an optional deadline:
  - hook path sets a tight budget (e.g., 10ms)
  - CLI tools may set a larger budget or none

### Budget application

- Always check deadline between stages:
  - after JSON parse
  - after normalization
  - after execution-context classification
  - before/after each expensive tier (heredoc extraction, AST matching)

- For expensive tiers:
  - if remaining time is low, skip deep analysis and fail-open (ALLOW/WARN) with a trace/log marker

### External processes (if used)

- Any spawned process (ast-grep) must have a hard timeout:
  - kill child
  - treat as "analysis skipped" (fail-open)

## Output / observability

- If a deadline is exceeded:
  - hook mode: ALLOW (fail-open) + structured log (if enabled)
  - explain mode: show `skipped_due_to_budget: true`

## Acceptance criteria

- Hook evaluation never exceeds the configured budget by more than a small overhead margin.
- Budget overruns are detectable via logs and explain traces.
- No panics or hangs when budgets are exceeded.

## Tests

- Deterministic test that forces the budget to be tiny (e.g., 0us) and asserts:
  - evaluation returns ALLOW in hook mode
  - explain includes the budget-skip marker

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-99e.3.1` shared evaluator
- `git_safety_guard-99e.11` regex safety audit (mitigate un-preemptable regex worst cases)
- `git_safety_guard-1gt.1.1` trace model (to surface budget skips cleanly)
","notes":"Partial progress: Tier 2 heredoc extraction now enforces ExtractionLimits.timeout_ms (fail-open) and records SkipReason::Timeout. Also fixes misleading UnterminatedHeredoc attribution when max_body_lines/max_body_bytes stops body scanning (now reports ExceededLineLimit/ExceededSizeLimit as appropriate). Added unit tests for timeout + line-limit skip reasons.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:20:02.564887486-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T03:55:27.971316841-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.14","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:20:13.584359237-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.14","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:20:18.627347442-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.14","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:20:23.673397563-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.14","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:20:28.803796-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","title":"pre-commit: install/uninstall helper (git hooks)","description":"## Objective

Implement a helper command to install a pre-commit hook that runs `dcg scan --staged`.

## Requirements

- Idempotent install.
- Clear messaging about bypass (`--no-verify`).
- Support printing configs for hook managers (optional).

## Design notes

### Do not overwrite existing hooks silently

Pre-commit hooks are user-owned and often already exist.

Proposed behavior:

- If `.git/hooks/pre-commit` does not exist: create a minimal hook that runs `dcg scan --staged`.
- If it exists and was installed by `dcg` (detect via a sentinel comment): update in-place idempotently.
- If it exists and is *not* a `dcg` hook: do **not** overwrite; instead print explicit instructions for manual integration.

### Uninstall must be safe

- Only uninstall if the hook contains the `dcg` sentinel.
- Otherwise: refuse and explain (never delete/overwrite unknown user hooks).

### Exit codes / messaging

- The installed hook should:
  - surface scan output
  - fail the commit when `--fail-on` threshold is met
  - mention bypass path (`git commit --no-verify`) but encourage allowlisting / fixing

## Test plan

- E2E test in a temp git repo:
  - install when hook absent
  - install again (idempotent)
  - uninstall
  - attempt uninstall when non-dcg hook exists (should refuse)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg scan install-pre-commit` installs a working `.git/hooks/pre-commit` hook when none exists.
- Running install twice is a no-op (idempotent) and never duplicates hook lines.
- `dcg scan uninstall-pre-commit` removes only hooks it installed (sentinel-based) and refuses to touch other hooks.
- Hook messaging is clear about:
  - what failed
  - how to fix/allowlist
  - how to bypass (`--no-verify`) with explicit risk warning
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:55.636908818-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T19:48:36.091372303-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T19:48:36.091372303-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented `dcg scan install-pre-commit` + `uninstall-pre-commit` (sentinel-based, idempotent, refuses unknown hooks), refactored scan CLI to support optional subcommands, and added temp-git-repo unit tests for install/uninstall + CLI parsing/conflict coverage.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:19.453653841-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:10.086282153-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:15.185035321-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","title":"pre-commit: .dcg/hooks.toml config parsing","description":"## Objective

Support a project config file that controls `dcg scan` behavior for pre-commit usage.

This exists so teams can adopt scanning without requiring everyone to remember long CLI flag sets.

## Proposed config file

- Path: `.dcg/hooks.toml`
- Scope: repository (committed, code-reviewed)

## Config goals

- Make rollout conservative and low-noise by default.
- Provide a single place to encode:
  - what to scan
  - what to exclude
  - how to redact
  - when to fail

## Suggested schema (v1)

```toml
[scan]
fail_on = "error"           # error|warning|none
format = "json"             # pretty|json (pre-commit usually uses pretty)
max_file_size = 1000000
max_findings = 50
redact = "quoted"           # none|quoted|aggressive
truncate = 200

[scan.paths]
include = ["scripts/**", ".github/workflows/**", "Dockerfile", "Makefile"]
exclude = ["target/**", "node_modules/**"]
```

## Validation behavior

- Unknown keys produce warnings (do not crash hook).
- Invalid values produce clear errors in `dcg scan` / `dcg doctor`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg scan --staged` (pre-commit integration) can load `.dcg/hooks.toml` automatically.
- CLI flags override config file values (explicit beats implicit).
- Config parsing failures are actionable.

## Tests

- Golden tests for config parsing:
  - valid config
  - unknown keys warnings
  - invalid enum values
- Integration test: config affects include/exclude and redact behavior deterministically.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:00.729320573-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T20:00:01.847120088-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T20:00:01.847120088-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented repo-local scan config via .dcg/hooks.toml: added parser with unknown-key warnings + actionable errors, wired automatic loading (git toplevel) into dcg scan, refactored scan CLI flags to be optional so config can supply defaults, and added unit tests for parsing + merge/override behavior.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:24.545211146-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.281979873-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:25.375217044-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.3","title":"pre-commit: E2E fixture repo + detailed logging","description":"## Objective

Add an end-to-end test that:

- creates a small fixture repo
- stages files with known findings
- runs `dcg scan --staged`
- asserts output summary and exit code

Logging must include per finding:

- file:line
- extracted command
- rule id
- reason

## Design notes

- The fixture repo should include at least:
  - a safe file (no findings)
  - a file with an executable destructive command in an executed context
  - a file with a *data-only* mention of a destructive string (should not be flagged)
- The test should assert both:
  - correctness (findings exist where expected)
  - ergonomics (output is actionable and stable)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Test creates a temp git repo, stages files, runs `dcg scan --staged`, and asserts:
  - exit code matches `--fail-on` policy
  - output contains stable rule IDs and file:line locations
  - extracted commands are shown (redacted/truncated per policy)
- Test includes at least one negative fixture ensuring data-only mentions do not produce findings.
- Test output includes enough logging to debug failures without re-running locally.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:05.825341465-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T23:17:36.442086703-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T23:17:36.442086703-05:00","close_reason":"Added E2E test script: 21 tests covering empty staged, destructive shell, data-only files, mixed files, fail-on policy, JSON schema, deterministic ordering, and GitHub Actions extraction","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:29.635035168-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:30.532356101-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:35.79204859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","title":"pre-commit: docs + recommended rollout","description":"## Objective

Document how to adopt `dcg scan` in a pre-commit workflow **safely** (low-noise rollout that preserves developer/agent velocity).

## Required doc sections

### 1) What this is (and is not)

- `dcg` hook protects *interactive execution*.
- `dcg scan` protects repos by scanning *executable contexts* in files.
- Emphasize: extractor-based scanning (NOT naive grep) to avoid false positives.

### 2) Recommended rollout plan (warn-first)

- Start with:
  - narrow include set (workflows + Dockerfiles + Makefiles)
  - `--fail-on error` only
  - warnings informational for 1–2 weeks

- Expand gradually:
  - add `.sh` scripts
  - add additional extractors (compose, Terraform, GitLab CI, package.json)

### 3) How to interpret findings

- Meaning of `decision` and `severity`.
- How to read `rule_id` and why it is stable.

### 4) How to fix a finding

- Run `dcg explain "..."` for the extracted command.
- Prefer changing code to safer alternatives.
- If it is a false positive:
  - use `dcg allow` / `dcg allowlist add ...` (project or user)
  - explain allowlist safety constraints (expiration, risk_ack)

### 5) Privacy / secrets

- Recommend redaction defaults.
- Avoid printing full extracted commands in CI logs.

### 6) Example configurations

- `.dcg/hooks.toml` example for staged scanning.
- Example pre-commit hook snippet (and optional managers: husky/lefthook).

## Acceptance criteria

- A user can adopt scan-precommit without reading source.
- Docs explicitly warn against turning on warning-as-fail immediately.
- Docs show exactly how to resolve false positives safely.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:10.920878487-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:30:51.834974021-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:34.725455653-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:41.044919711-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:46.278526462-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- A user can adopt scan-precommit without reading source.
- Docs explicitly warn against turning on warning-as-fail immediately.
- Docs show exactly how to resolve false positives safely.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","title":"CI integration for dcg scan (PR diff scanning + PR summary)","description":"## Objective

Provide a "team-wide" safety net by running `dcg scan` in CI for pull requests.

## Background

Local hooks are not always installed or are bypassed (`--no-verify`). CI is where:

- the org can enforce safety rules
- results can be reviewed centrally
- rollouts can start in warn-only mode (avoid blocking velocity)

## Proposed MVP (GitHub Actions)

### 1) Workflow snippet (no marketplace action yet)

- A documented GitHub Actions workflow that:
  - checks out full history (diff computation)
  - builds `dcg` (or downloads release artifact later)
  - runs `dcg scan --git-diff <base>...HEAD --format json --fail-on error`
  - uploads the JSON artifact

### 2) Human-readable PR summary

- Render a concise Markdown summary to the job log (and optionally as a PR comment):
  - top N findings, grouped by file
  - per finding: line, extracted command (truncated), rule id, reason, suggestion
  - totals by severity (error/warn/info)

### 3) Rollout model

- Default recommendation:
  - start with `--fail-on error` only
  - treat warnings as advisory for 1-2 weeks
  - tighten after allowlists are created

## Output requirements

- JSON schema must be stable:
  - include `file`, `line`, `extractor_id`, `extracted_command`, `decision`, `rule_id`, `reason`, `suggestion?`
- CI mode should have deterministic ordering.

## Security/privacy

- Provide a `--redact` option (or config) for commands that might contain secrets.
- CI should avoid printing entire extracted commands if they are very long.

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg scan --git-diff ...` handles empty diffs cleanly (0 findings, exit 0).
- A workflow snippet exists in docs or templates and is proven to run in this repo.
- When errors exist, CI fails with an actionable summary.
- When warnings exist (and `--fail-on error`), CI passes but prints the warnings clearly.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- CI should default to diff-only scanning and warn-first rollout.
- PR comments must clearly show extracted executed commands so reviewers can judge signal.

### Tests
- `git_safety_guard-scan.5.3` covers `--git-diff` integration behavior.
- Add at least one end-to-end CI fixture that includes:
  - a true positive in an executed context
  - a data-only dangerous string that must not be flagged","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:31.299265569-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:28:51.436024163-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:40.548508411-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:47.19945344-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:52.295002514-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.1","title":"CI: workflow template for PR diff scanning","description":"## Objective

Provide a copy/paste GitHub Actions workflow snippet for:

- building (or downloading) `dcg`
- running `dcg scan --git-diff <base>...HEAD --format json --fail-on error`
- uploading artifacts

## Acceptance criteria

- Documented and tested in this repo.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Provide a default `--fail-on error` policy (warn-only for warnings).
- Make it easy to scope to diff-only files to avoid noise on large repos.

### Tests
- Validate the workflow in `git_safety_guard-scan.5.3` by running against a fixture repo in CI.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:47.608110165-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T22:04:35.42538357-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T22:04:35.42538357-05:00","close_reason":"Added .github/workflows/scan.yml for PR diff scanning in CI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:19:07.365697932-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:00.560850741-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","title":"CI: optional PR comment renderer (top findings)","description":"## Objective

Optionally render a concise Markdown summary suitable for PR comments.

## Requirements

- Input: scan JSON schema v1.
- Output:
  - grouped by file
  - severity-first ordering (error, warning, info)
  - include per finding:
    - file:line
    - rule_id
    - short reason
    - truncated/redacted command preview
    - suggestion (if available)

## Markdown UX

- Use `<details><summary>` blocks per file to keep comments compact.
- Hard cap output size:
  - top N findings overall
  - per-file top M

## Determinism

- Sorting rules are deterministic and documented.

## Acceptance criteria

- Renderer output is deterministic and test-covered.
- Renderer never prints unredacted full commands when redaction is enabled.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:52.707720678-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T22:09:47.542128698-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T22:09:47.542128698-05:00","close_reason":"Added --format markdown option for PR comment output with <details> blocks, severity badges, and truncated command previews","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:19:12.458474915-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:05.839049463-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:11.120494138-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
